rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH KARNAZES VS. SAINT PAUL TRAVELERS INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 20, 2012, Line 1. DEFENDANTS TOM COLLINS, HARRY GILBERT, ST. PAUL SURPLUS LINES INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action and with fifteen days leave to amend as to the third through seventh causes of action, and overruled as to all other causes of action challenged by the demurrer. As to the first cause of action, plaintiff alleges no facts to support the existence of a fiduciary relationship and being aware of a lien does not create that relationship. The fraud causes of action need to be pled with greater specificity including the allegation of ultimate facts showing justifiable reliance. The statute of limitations argument lacks merit. The San Mateo documents do not show that the claims are time barred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH KARNAZES VS. SAINT PAUL TRAVELERS INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 20, 2012, Line 1. DEFENDANT TOM COLLINS, HARRY GILBERT, ST. PAUL SURPLUS LINES INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The thirteenth cause of action is stricken without leave to amend and the request for punitive damages is stricken with fifteen days leave to amend. The thirteenth cause of action is duplicative of the tenth cause of action. The request for punitive damages is supported only by conclusory allegations. rulings.Case Title: KATELYN TAM VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 20, 2012, Line 17. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production. Stating something in the Conditions of Admission form, even if it is signed by the patient, does not make it true. Defendant did not present any evidence that the two doctors were not the agents of defendant Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. KIMOMEX MARKERTS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 20, 2012, LINE 1, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO POST-JUDGMENT DISCOVERY AND FOR AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED with respect to judgment debtors Kinomex Markets, Inc. and Brian Fetters. Kinomex Markets and Brian Fetters shall respond to Plaintiff's Post Judgment Interrogatories and Plaintiff's Post Judgment Demand for Production of Documents without objection within 15 days of service of notice of entry of this order. DENIED with respect to judgment debtor Albert Tobias. The proof of service of the post-judgment discovery on Mr. Tobias Lujan states that the post-judgment discovery was served by mail, but does not reflect that the discovery was served upon him at his address of record in this action. Request for sanctions is GRANTED IN PART. Kinomex Markets shall pay Plaintiff $170 within 15 days. Brian Fetters shall pay Plaintiff $170 within 15 days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA L. PETERSON VS. US BANCORP DBA US BANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Evidence Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 20, 2012, LINE 2, DEFENDANT U.S. BANCORP'S MOTION FOR EVIDENCE SANCTIONS. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. It would be helpful to the Court for the parties to be prepared to discuss their positions regarding specific language for an order concerning the evidence that should be excluded for Plaintiff''s failure to obey the Court's April 2, 2012 order compelling further responses to Form Interrogatories 2.2(c), 6.6, 6.7(a), 8.3, 8.4, 8.5, 8.7, 8.8, 9.2, 10.1, 10.3, 11.1, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.6 and 50.1 through 50.6. The parties should be prepared to also discuss whether the Court's January 18, 2012 order setting a due date of March 31, 2012 for Plaintiff's responses to Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set One and Demand for Production, Set One is an order that qualifies as a necessary prerequisite for the imposition of evidence sanctions rather than issuance of an order compelling further responses and production of documents. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN ZUCKERT et al VS. RUSSELL HARRY MURPHY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 20, 2012, Line 8. DEFENDANT RUSSELL MURPHY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Plaintiff alleges misrepresentations of fact. For example, paragraphs 42-44 of the Amended Complaint allege that defendant falsely represented that the work was compliant with the building code. An individual defendant is liable for his own wrongful act even if he was acting as the corporation's representative. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
